data.title: Hercules Capital: Last Man Standing As Internal BDCs Move To Monthly Dividends
data.description:
Hercules Capital (HTGC) is paying out a well-covered double-digit dividend yield that could be moved to a monthly payment schedule in line with its peers. While there hasn't yet been official guidance from the business development company ("BDC") that it intends to move to a monthly distribution schedule, the direction of the wind is clear. To be clear, there are dozens of BDCs, but only five are internally managed. Main Street Capital (MAIN), Capital Southwest (CSWC), Trinity Capital (TRIN), SuRo Capital (SSSS), and HTGC. SSSS is an equity-focused BDC with lumpy dividends that reflect exits on its venture-capital portfolio. Hence, there is really only four internally managed BDCs for income investors, and most have switched to monthly dividend distributions.

HTGC's most direct venture-debt focused peer, TRIN, was the most recent BDC to switch to monthly dividends. This essentially leaves HTGC as the last man standing, a position that could very well see the BDC make the switch to a monthly distribution this year. HTGC last declared a quarterly base cash dividend of $0.40 per share, kept unchanged from its prior distribution, and $1.60 per share annualized for an 8.5% dividend yield. There was also a $0.07 per share supplemental dividend, which means a roughly 1.5% incremental yield when annualized. HTGC has been consistent with paying out an aggreagte $0.47 per share quarterly distribution, with the current 10% dividend yield providing for a 573 basis points spread to the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y). The CME FedWatch Tool currently has 50 basis points of rate cuts through 2026 as its base case for the year at a 32.1% probability.

Common Share Dividend Coverage And The Baby Bonds

I last rated HTGC as a buy, with this sentiment unchanged on the back of a possibly slower pace of Fed rate cuts in 2026, coverage of the double-digit yield, and continued robust access to institutional capital. The BDC realized fiscal 2025 third quarter net investment income ("NII") of $88.6 million, or $0.49 per share, to provide 104% coverage of the aggregate dividend payment. HTGC's coverage of the base dividends is significantly larger at 122%. The BDC's total investments at fair value were $4.31 billion, up 17.8% from $3.66 billion at the start of the year. This growth reflects positive cadence with originations, with the third quarter seeing originations of around $846 million to drive record originations of $2.87 billion for the first 3 quarters of 2025. HTGC is well on track to outpace its 2024 total of $3.12 billion.

Total investment income came in at a new record at $138.1 million, growth of roughly 10.3% year-over-year as assets under management ("AUM") closed out the third quarter at $5.5 billion. AUM was also up 20.7% over its year-ago comp. Critically, HTGC is notching records with its core metrics to form a moat against what's set to be a more Darwinistic backdrop for BDCs from a declining Fed funds rate environment. The BDC's investment portfolio is highly sensitive as it's built with 97.8% floating rate loans, albeit with interest rate floors. As there were another 50 basis points of rate cuts in the fourth quarter, HTGC should expect to see an incremental $0.05 per share hit to earnings. However, the dampening effects of the rate cuts would only be fully reflected in the BDC's fiscal 2026 first quarter.

Assuming the BDC's portfolio remained static, coverage of the base and aggregate dividend could drop significantly. This means HTGC's public trading 6.25% Notes due 10/30/2033 (HCXY) form the only other public security for prospective investors looking to build a position in HTGC, but without the risk of slipping dividend coverage. These pay out a $1.56 annual coupon per note for a 6.14% yield on cost as they're currently trading hands for $25.39 per share ahead of their 30th of October 2033 maturity. The commons still, in my opinion, provide a better risk and reward profile with the income substantial enough and current coverage set to be bolstered by ongoing record originations.

Conclusion

HTGC had 2 of its portfolio companies on nonaccrual at the end of the third quarter with a cost of $52.2 million, around 1.2% of its total investment portfolio at cost and far lower than the BDC average of 2.3%. If the BDC can keep its nonaccruals below the industry average at cost, continue to ramp up originations in 2026, and transition to a monthly dividend, the ticker should capture a large share of a possible liquidity flight from BDCs that have not capably scaled their portfolio to handle the current macroeconomic backdrop of falling rates. I am rating HTGC as a buy.

data.title: PENN Entertainment: The Market Gave Up, But Maybe Too Early
data.description:
I like the gaming industry, especially when it's more closely linked to real estate. I have a position in VICI Properties (VICI), I'm bullish on Red Rock Resorts (RRR), and more recently, I decided to look at PENN Entertainment (PENN), which, despite not being as solid as VICI, as it's not a REIT but rather an operator, is also a case that intrigued me greatly. But it's worth mentioning that this is a very different case from the others. At first glance, it seems to be a terrible case, as there's some “confusion” related to the digital strategy, a large loss, and the valuation seems to indicate a value trap, with a P/B below 1x.

But still, looking deeper into the case, I don't think it's as bad as the market seems to suggest. We're still talking about a company with several casinos around the US that manages to deliver minimally interesting results. In addition, management is trying to adjust strategies to remain relevant in both the physical and digital worlds.

Therefore, despite the relevant risks, I consider PENN stock a cautious buy.

PENN Entertainment: Starting with the Bad News

Before talking about the potential that PENN may eventually unlock, it's necessary to understand why this stock seems so cheap. A few quarters ago, the main strategy seemed to be digital expansion, with iGaming and sports betting, and one of the ways to achieve this was through an agreement with Disney's (DIS) ESPN.

More recently, PENN withdrew from the agreement with ESPN, explaining that it was difficult to scale ESPN bet:

“Although we made significant progress in improving our product offering and building a cohesive ecosystem with ESPN, we were unable to establish ESPN BET as a scale player. It is the right time to realign our interactive focus, prioritizing our digital assets in Canada and our Hollywood iCasino product to further leverage our core retail casino business and overall omnichannel business model. This shift emphasizes cross-sell opportunities across our ecosystem and enhances connectivity with our 33 million-plus PENN Play customer database.”

In part, this is also related to the competitive environment. Try to think of some form/alternative of betting on sports. There are several. There's DraftKings (DKNG) and other large listed companies, and we can also think of alternatives such as Polymarket.

This, in itself, was something that the market penalized, and rightly so. The company has casinos and hotels, but the main focus was on the digital scale, which is a growing market but extremely competitive. But beyond that, the execution and confusion of this strategy were also penalized. The partnership with ESPN not only went wrong, but they also failed to deliver anywhere near what they were aiming for, which was a much more substantial market share.

In other words, it's as if there is now a lack of confidence in PENN Entertainment's execution. There's still an important physical infrastructure, but it's practically ignored by the market since one of the main focuses of management is still digital, and recently the execution has not brought good results.

But that's only part of the uncertainty because if you treat digital as an optionality, if it works, great; if not, it doesn't matter to the core business. However, this is also reflected in the numbers. For example, the net loss for Q3 was $865 million, and most of that was impairment losses, which in that quarter amounted to $825 million. And most of that impairment is related to the “interactive” segment.

This reflects that the company invested heavily in this and now recognizes that the investments were not so well made. Another reflection of the digital “disaster” is that, in addition to low revenue, adjusted EBITDA was negative at $76 million, and the company itself called this “below expectations” in its release.

All of this worsens the perception of the company's capital structure, which has a net debt of ~$2.1 billion, while operating income, excluding impairment and depreciation, is only ~$160 million per quarter, i.e., it's a leverage of more than 3x even after adjusting this operating income.

Why I Still See Value in PENN Entertainment

But even in this very challenging context, to say the least, I still see some value in PENN. The first point is that most of the revenue, and especially EBITDA, comes from its physical structure. Therefore, this is the core of the case, and it's the main part that should weigh in an assessment.

The interactive segment was poor, as mentioned, but in Q3, “Retail Property” revenue was $1.4 billion, with a slight improvement YoY, with the company mentioning that “demand was generally stable” in the core. Adjusted EBITDAR for this segment was also $465 million, with margins of 32%, meaning it's still a very profitable segment that can be called a cash cow.

Another point, already mentioned, is that the result looks very bad, but this is due to non-recurring items. YoY, we can see an increase in consolidated revenue, and the only item that was really “worrying” in expenses was impairment losses, which is not a recurring item.

If we remove these impairment losses and depreciation, we already have an operating income of $160 million for the quarter, or a capacity to generate something close to $640 million per year. And this is very much in line with the valuation. We're talking about a company that currently has only $1.8 billion in market cap, and even if we add what the company calls “traditional debt,” we still have an EV of $4 billion, which is an EV-to-OI of 6x. If you look at it from an accounting perspective, this EV would be much higher, since the “correct” thing to do would be to incorporate leases into this debt, which makes it much more inflated and causes it to exceed $10 billion, and even using this metric, it's still possible to find an interesting valuation for the case.

Another interesting metric is the P/B. I don't think it's such an important metric to anchor, but currently the P/B is below 1x, as you can see in the table below. It still seems to hold some downside, but at the same time, it shows the market's disbelief, which is basically saying that PENN is worth the same as what it has on its balance sheet.

Note that so far I haven't mentioned much about digital, because for me, it's not such a central pillar. But still, the company needs to at least improve this segment so that the core can move forward. And with this prospect, I can be minimally optimistic. In the image below, PENN shows that retail customers are increasing penetration and contributing to digital growth. So, thinking about it this way, digital becomes something more complementary to the core, a way to monetize the same customer more.

In addition, the company is managing to significantly grow its digital database, with more than 5 million customers. It seems quite feasible to me that in a second phase, with a certain amount of operational leverage, the company will at least be able to stabilize operations and extract the minimum profitability from this business, which, together with a healthy core, would be enough to unlock a rerating for PENN stock.

The Bottom Line: Not Easy, But Asymmetrical

This subtitle sums up what I think of the PENN case. I don't think it's an easy case. In fact, I think management still has a lot to prove in terms of execution, whether they will be able to stabilize in the digital space, and whether the core can maintain minimally healthy growth. But I also think that the valuation today is asymmetrical. The downside seems low, supported by a compressed P/B ratio and low multiples, and if execution is at least reasonable, it is already possible to glimpse some upside.

Therefore, my rating for PENN is a cautious buy.

data.title: Silver: What's Different This Time?
data.description:
Silver has historically traded as an extremely volatile commodity, plagued with a high level of speculation. Accordingly, market tops have traditionally been followed by violent downside moves into bear market territory, as illustrated in the chart below.

Currently silver is trading at an all-time high near $95, deep into overbought territory. The looming question is whether the current price of silver is topping, soon to be followed by a retreat as shown by the chart in 1980 and 2011.

In 2026, however, there are multiple factors that should portend a much different outcome than in past bull markets, discussed below.

Silver shortage

Silver has experienced a 5-year physical deficit with mine production well under demand, documented by The Silver Institute World Silver Survey. During the last five years, demand has exceeded supply by 100 to 200 million ounces. It is noteworthy that mine production in 2011 was significantly higher in 2011 at 1160 million ounces compared to 2024 production of 819.7 million ounces reported in the World Silver Survey. From the above numbers, it is apparent that there has been underinvestment in mining, which could limit silver production for a decade or more.

A major problem with supply is due to the fact that it takes about 5-10 years to explore, develop, and permit a new silver mine. In addition, there are only a handful of mines that are primary silver producers. Most silver is produced as a byproduct of mining other minerals like gold, copper, zinc, and lead. Consequently, about 70% of silver is mined as a byproduct that is limited by the mining of other minerals.

In the interim, only increased recycling can add to supply significantly. However, there is only a limited amount of above-ground silver available due to widespread use in electronic devices that are not recycled.

Rough estimates of above-ground silver range between about 6-15 billion ounces in everything from electronic devices to silverware and jewelry. It's likely that recycling will increase markedly with increases in silver prices, but a looming question remains on how much silver can be recycled. For example, there are only a limited number of refineries and smelters in the U.S. positioned for recycling.

China export restrictions

Beyond the foregoing, a further reduction in silver supply will occur due to China placing restrictions on silver exports, requiring licensing to export the metal. China's action will likely have a big impact on world silver supply since it is the second largest producer of silver behind Mexico, documented by the World Silver Survey linked above.

In addition, silver is trading about $10 higher on the Shanghai futures exchange compared to the COMEX/US (shown on the line chart below), indicating extraordinarily high demand for silver in China.

Increasing demand

Increasing demand for silver is being fueled by exponential growth in electronics. Silver is the most electronically conductive commodity available and, accordingly, is being demanded widely in the ongoing growth of electrification of the world.

As noted in The World Silver Survey, solar panels (photovoltaics) alone are consuming nearly 200 million ounces annually. Beyond solar panels, electrification is growing exponentially in consumer electronics, electric vehicles, data centers, and countless other applications.

Recently it was disclosed that Samsung has developed a prototype solid-state battery pack for electric vehicles that will enable a driving range of about 600 miles with a charging time of about nine minutes. The battery pack will require up to a kilogram of silver (35.274 ounces) per battery pack. Simple arithmetic can illustrate the increase in demand for just a few thousand EVs deploying the Samsung battery pack.

World market for silver

The current world market for silver is far different from in historical bull markets. While most historical bull markets occurred primarily in the U.S., currently worldwide markets are increasing demand exponentially, primarily in China and India. Both countries are rapidly deploying solar panels to build electrical infrastructure to provide cleaner energy extending to remote areas where little or no electricity previously existed. Further, China is an industrial power far beyond what existed historically, while India continues to build its industrial base.

U.S. critical mineral designation

In addition to the foregoing, the United States designated silver as a critical mineral in November 2025. Accordingly, the U.S. will likely begin stockpiling silver, exerting additional pressure on available supply. It should be pointed out that a significant amount of silver is used in military hardware, especially advanced military hardware like missile systems. Interestingly, military demand is not included in the World Survey.

Currency debasement

As the U.S. continues to print money, many investors hedge their portfolios with hard assets that do not debase, like silver. The chart below illustrates growth in the M2 money supply, which has been accelerating in recent years. Silver, as both a precious and monetary metal, provides a more than ample hedge to currency debasement.

Investment demand

Investment demand for silver is increasing, which is apparent in the price movement to all time highs. It is also apparent that few generalist investors have precious metals in their portfolios. Accordingly, some investment advisors have begun to recommend a portion of their client's portfolios include commodities, including predious metals like gold and silver, documented in a Goldman Sachs report. Since this trend has only recently begun, it is likely there much more upside to investors moving into the precious metals space.

Geopolitical risks

Recent years have witnessed a rapid increase in geopolitical turbulence with wars, tariff wars, and other world-wide turbulance. Elevated geopolitical risks will likely continue for several years, unpinning demand for safe-haven assets like gold and silver.

Investing in silver

There are two primary ways to invest in silver--investing in ETFs tied to the pride of silver and investing in silver mining stocks. Two key ETFs that track the price of silver are the iShares Silver Trust (SLV) and the Sprott Physical Silver Trust (PSLV).

There are two key silver miners ETFs, Global X Silver Miners ETF (SIL), and Amplify Junior Silver Miners ETF (SILJ). While the junior miners carry more risk, they also have more upside potential due somewhat to buyout prospects.

Investing in individual mining stocks should be advoided by investors unfamiliar with the mining industry. Without a thorough knowledge of how to analyze mining company prospects, it should be left to professionals. A more thorough discussion of the foregoing is provided in a previous SA article.

Risks

Silver has been an extremely volatile commodity with wide price movement both to the upside and downside. Investors should anticipate wide price swings both before and after investing in the silver market. Investing in silver is not for the "faint of heart."

Likely outcome for silver and final thoughts

With silver trading near all-time highs, $95 at this writing, there is reason to speculate that silver is long overdue for a significant correction as it approaches the $100 mark. Once silver breaks through the $100 mark, there is likely to be significant selling with a $100 selling target. While a correction can come at any time, it is likely to be short-lived due to unprecedented demand for physical silver. While futures markets previously determined the price of silver, physical demand has taken command of the market.

News is spreading of tight silver supplies and it is likely some industrial players are taking notice with purchases to ensure their supply. Without silver, many industrial products just cannot be manufactured. Indeed Samsung has contracted with Silver Storm Mining, locking in a 2-year supply. Others are likely to follow.

data.title: Federal Reserve Watch: Steady As She Goes
data.description:
Beginning in December 2025, the Federal Reserve has overseen a rise of just over $42 billion in its portfolio of securities held outright.

So the switch between a monetary policy of "quantitative tightening" to one of "quantitative easing" has taken place.

The move has been a modest one. That is, the increase in the securities portfolio has occurred over eight weeks.

The "excess reserves" in the banking system...that is, the Reserve Balances with Federal Reserve Banks...have risen by $58.5 billion, to $2,956.5 billion.

And the effective Federal Funds rate, the Fed's policy rate of interest, has been moved just once during this period of quantitative easing, although it has been dropped three times in the past year.

The Federal Reserve, under the leadership of Fed Chairman Jerome Powell, has been lowering the Fed's policy rate of interest, as long as the FOMC believes that market conditions warrant such a move, moves that can be sustained over time.

Market participants believe that the Fed will lower its policy rate at least one more time this year...possibly twice...but the leadership at the Fed has cautioned that they really don't want to get ahead of market conditions in reducing the policy rate.

Market attention in recent weeks has been drawn to the expansion of the M2 money stock.

The M2 money stock has been growing again since the middle of 2023, but real attention actually extends back to early 2020, since this was the time when the Federal Reserve was expanding the money stock in order to fight off the negative effects of the COVID-19 pandemic and its following recession.

Since December 2019, the M2 money stock has been growing at a compound rate of growth of 6.4 percent year-over-year.

The concern is that if the real economy is growing at a compound rate of growth close to 6.4 percent, year-over-year, then the economy could expect price inflation to come in around 4.0 percent, given that the economy would be growing around 2.4 percent, or so.

This rate of inflation is outside the range the Federal Reserve would like to maintain over time.

Given these figures, one can argue that the Federal Reserve still has some work to do.

That is, current monetary policy, which includes all the massive inflow of M2 money stock in 2020 through 2022, still threatens to produce an inflation in the American economy that is greater than the policymakers would like.

Consequently, up until this period of time, it has been argued that the Federal Reserve still has work to do to get the rate of inflation down from current levels.

In other words, the Federal Reserve cannot be too aggressive in "re-starting" its monetary easing, or it will reignite inflationary pressures.

Some analysts are arguing that the Fed cannot "back off" its efforts to return inflation to levels more consistent with the Fed's long-term goals for getting the inflation rate back to 2.0 percent or below.

Recent statements about monetary policy argue that giving up the battle to reposition the economy at this time would be too bad because the Fed has carefully spent three and three-quarters years fighting the inflationary pressures.

Giving up now would be a waste of the "good efforts" of the Fed to bring inflation under control.

Some analysts are pointing to the gold market and the dollar market to make this case.

In recent weeks, the price of gold has risen very rapidly, reaching numerous "new" historic highs.

It has been argued that this movement into gold is reflective of the investor concern that the U.S. government will not support the U.S. price system.

This feeling has also spread into the foreign exchange markets and put the value of the U.S. dollar under question for the same reasons. Expectations are for a continued decline in the value of the dollar.

And these situations are getting all tied up with the discussion about what President Trump might do to the Federal Reserve leadership and Federal Reserve independence.

There is even a worry about whether or not Trump might change the "style" of how the Fed does business.

Right now, the Federal Reserve has moved on to a monetary policy of quantitative easing.

It appears that the Fed will continue to increase the size of the Fed's securities portfolio modestly...on a regular basis...and will continue to consider further reductions in the Fed's policy rate of interest.

However, if inflation shows signs that it is not going away, the Fed leadership will not continue to reduce the Federal Funds rate.

Chairman Powell will cease to be the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System in May.

We don't know what will happen after that.

Investors, however, should be open to changing their understanding of how monetary policy is being conducted.

data.title: Reddit Needs To Grow Like Meta While Facing Tougher Challenges
data.description:
Performance Assessment

Reddit, Inc. (RDDT) has performed in-line with the S&P500 since my last update on the stock:

So my bullish view has not really worked out. But the good thing is that it has also not led to underperformance.

Thesis

My view on Reddit is not as optimistic this time:

  1. Global social media bans are a big threat.
  2. High dependency on Google for traffic is a risk.
  3. The bullish advertising revenues thesis is playing out.
  4. Valuations seem exorbitant.
  5. RDDT looks like it can go lower.

Global Social Media Bans Are A Big Threat

Social media is getting more addictive. And there is an ever-growing body of research warning about the detrimental impacts of that addiction on mental health and productivity. Last year, some governments, such as Denmark, started to plan for bans on social media access for youth. My country of Australia has led the way in becoming the first nation to ban social media for teens in late Dec'25. And other countries such as New Zealand, Malaysia, the UK and more are expected to follow as curbing social media access to youth is becoming a worldwide trend.

Unfortunately for Reddit, it is one of the platforms affected by the ban. And although there are some legal challenges to the bans on the grounds of free speech, I believe this is still a headwind because the issue goes beyond the legal bans, in my opinion. What we must not ignore is the growing awareness of the risks of social media addiction, which, I believe, is likely to lead to more efforts to curb access and usage one way or another.

For Reddit, the impact would be a hit to the daily active user metrics:

This is bad because less users means less content, which can itself make the platform less active and reduce aggregate user times overall. And advertisers would then find lower value from the platform, hindering revenue growth on that front (which is the dominant driver for Reddit):

High Dependency On Google And AI Chatbots For Traffic Is A Risk

I found this exchange quite revealing in the Q3 FY25 earnings call:

I guess, Steve, more than anything else, you've talked about 50% of your traffic comes direct and 50% from Google...

- Richard Greenfield from LightShed Partners LLC

I'd say those numbers are approximate, but pretty close...

- CEO Steven Huffman

And I don't think this is just about Google (GOOGL) (GOOG) as a lot of traffic comes from sources cited in AI chatbots too. Now, some analysts believe that this is advantageous for Reddit, arguing that mere citation is enough to drive traffic:

If AI answers compress publisher traffic, then the new economic 'winners' become the sources AI answers choose to quote. Multiple studies show that citations disproportionately mention Reddit.

- Needham Analysts

But I am rather skeptical about this view. Here's why:

I remember in the early days of Google, people used to browse multiple pages to get the answers they wanted. But then this gradually changed; it was either you found the search result you wanted or were interested in on the first page, or you amended your search and tried again. I don't know if you've noticed, but the kids who grew up with the internet and social media tend to not even scroll down the first page much; if nothing is click-worthy from the initial glance, the search is revised.

Given these trends, I think after the initial novelty of clicking on sources in AI chatbot answers that may route them to the underlying Reddit threads, people are evolving to value speed and convenience again. Indeed, ask yourself if it is often enough for you to know that AI chatbot answers are coming from somewhere without you needing to go to the source and check.

Hence, I believe the AI chatbots are more likely to reduce traffic overall to the Reddit threads, especially as AI overview answers summarize content and give immediate answers directly at the top of the results page:

Indeed, I believe that as AI chatbots get better, the incentive for users to read directly from sources would be further reduced. In any case, I would be very keen to know if my hypothesis on user behavior here is true. So please do let me know about how you browse and whether or not AI chatbots or Google's AI summaries are reducing your time spent on underlying source sites.

The Bullish Advertising Revenues Thesis Is Playing Out

In my last article, I had expected Reddit to deliver 55% YoY revenue growth in Q3 FY25. But the company did much better at 68% YoY. This was due to strong advertising performance, which grew 74% YoY. Unsurprising because I believe this part of my earlier bullish thesis on RDDT stock is still playing out well:

Reddit is well poised to monetize on advertising in a big way.

- Hunting Alpha

I admit this is a bullish point for Reddit. Yet, I fear that this thesis driver may be a little weaker than a few months ago due to the traffic threats from social media bans and consumers' proclivity to actually click on the sources in their AI search queries. Management has already guided toward revenue deceleration toward 54% for Q4 FY25. In a couple of weeks, we will know for sure in the next earnings call.

Valuations Seem Exorbitant

RDDT stock's 1-yr fwd PE is high at 62.1x. This makes it the 2nd most richly valued stock among a broad set of similar social media platform peers:

In the current fundamentals' context, I believe the >4x premium to the sectoral median multiple of 15.2x is exorbitant. But let's go deeper and figure out what the current valuation really implies via a reverse earnings expectations analysis (done in the same style as a reverse DCF). This is the formula for the implied CAGR growth rate:

Taking a risk-free rate of 4.27% and an equity risk premium of 4.40%, I get an implied US market return of 8.67%. Let's be a bit generous and say RDDT stock will join the league of Meta (META) and command a 21x terminal PE multiple after 10 years. I think 10 years gives Reddit a reasonable amount of time to achieve steady-state valuations and economics.

The implied growth asks then is a 28.7% 10-year earnings CAGR, starting from a base of $349 million. Such a high, sustained growth rate for a decade, starting from those kinds of earnings base or higher, is extremely rare. Among social media companies, I believe only Meta has achieved that feat. This means the market is pricing RDDT like another META. And note that this is just to earn market-level returns. To beat market returns, RDDT needs to grow earnings at an even faster clip. So, looking at it this way, I claim that RDDT's valuations are prohibitively exorbitant for buys.

RDDT Looks Like It Can Go Lower

If this is your first time reading a Hunting Alpha article using Technical Analysis, you may want to read this post, which explains how and why I read the charts the way I do. All my charts reflect total shareholder return as they are adjusted for dividends/distributions.

Relative Read of RDDT vs SPX500

RDDT stock is failing to break new highs vs the S&P500 index as you can see from 3 rejections from a key monthly resistance level. The current month is potentially printing a strong bearish engulfing candle. I think this is a reliable indicator of further downside and hence underperformance ahead.

Takeaway & Positioning

I am starting to have a more pessimistic outlook on Reddit due to the growing trend of youth social media bans, which can endanger daily active user growth. I am also concerned that internet users' interactions with Google Search or other AI chatbots may reduce clicks to underlying source sites such as Reddit, thus taking traffic away. My earlier bullish view on advertising revenues still looks healthy. But I think this is notwithstanding increased risks, as active user metrics on the platform may take a quarter or two to degrade. Because this can make advertisers reassess the value of Reddit as a platform to attract valuable users.

On the valuations side, RDDT stock is valued at more than 4x the social media sectoral comps' median multiples. And a reverse earnings expectations analysis says Reddit will have to perform like Meta to justify its current valuations. The problem is, to grow at the ask rate of almost 30% CAGR over 10 years, Meta did not have to deal with the kind of regulatory and traffic cannibalization challenges to the same extent as what Reddit is facing now.

Charts-wise, RDDT stock looks rather weak to me and ready to break lower.

So all things considered, I am flipping my stance: 'Underperform/Sell'

How To Interpret Hunting Alpha's Ratings:

Strong Outperform/Strong Buy: Expect the company to outperform the S&P500 on a total shareholder return basis, with higher than usual confidence. I also have a net long position in the security in my personal portfolio.

Outperform/Buy: Expect the company to outperform the S&P500 on a total shareholder return basis.

Market Perform/Hold: Expect the company to perform in-line with the S&P500 on a total shareholder return basis.

Underperform/Sell: Expect the company to underperform the S&P500 on a total shareholder return basis.

Strong Underperform/Strong Sell: Expect the company to underperform the S&P500 on a total shareholder return basis, with higher than usual confidence.

The typical time-horizon for my views is multiple quarters to more than a year. It is not set in stone. However, I will share updates on my changes in stance in a pinned comment to this article and may also publish a new article discussing the reasons for the change in view.

data.title: GUSH: Examining The Structure And Suitability Of This 2X Leveraged ETF
data.description:
The Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF (GUSH) is a leveraged exchange-traded fund that seeks to provide its investors with 200% of the daily performance of the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index. This index is part of S&P Global’s Select Industry Indices family of equity indices, and it is designed to track the equity prices and yields of American companies that are engaged in the production or refining of crude oil, natural gas, natural gas liquids, and other hydrocarbon products.

As a leveraged ETF, and as the name of the fund implies, the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF only claims to deliver 200% of the price movement of the index on a day-to-day basis. It is not designed to be held overnight, and indeed, investors who do hold it overnight could see a very different performance than they expect. Indeed, as is the case with most leveraged exchange-traded funds, holding this one for an extended period of time could very easily result in the loss of much or all of your initial capital, even if the index generally rises over the period. Once again, the fund does pretty well at delivering on its objective when bought and sold within the same day, but it is intended for traders who are looking to capitalize on intraday price movements, not for long-term investors. More information about risks associated with leveraged ETFs such as GUSH can be found on the SEC's investor bulletin here.

Important Information About the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF

As stated in the introduction, the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF is a leveraged exchange-traded fund that is designed to deliver double the daily performance of the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index. Thus, whenever the index goes up 1% in a day, we can expect this fund to go up 2% on the same day. The reverse is also true, as this fund can be expected to decline 2% on any day that the index declines 1%. In this way, the fund’s movements are exactly the opposite of those of the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bear 2X Shares ETF (DRIP).

However, it is important to note that the fund’s performance is not necessarily going to be double that of the index over an extended period of time. GUSH has an inception date of May 28, 2015. Over the period from the fund’s inception date to January 21, 2026, the index has declined by 31.62%, whereas GUSH has declined a whopping 99.88%:

For the purpose of constructing this chart, I used the State Street SPDR S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production ETF (XOP), which is an index fund that tracks the same index as GUSH uses as its baseline.

Obviously, in the above chart, the index is represented by the black line, and the GUSH ETF is represented by the blue line.

The difference between GUSH and the index becomes even more apparent when we look at the five-year period that ended on January 21, 2026:

Once again, in this chart, the index is represented by the black line, whereas the GUSH ETF is represented by the blue line.

Here we immediately see the performance differences between the fund and the index. Over the five-year period that ended on January 21, 2026, the index rose 72.28%, but the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF only rose 48.79%. Thus, it clearly did not deliver double the performance of the index over the full five-year period.

However, we can see that GUSH was fairly successful at delivering double the price movement of the index on a day-to-day basis. On days on which the index rose, the ETF rose significantly more, and on days on which the index declined, the ETF declined significantly more. Thus, it is achieving its stated objective of delivering double the daily price movements of the index. However, this is not applicable over longer periods of time, and investors should not expect it to do so.

The Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF is the only exchange-traded fund on the market that aims to deliver 200% of the daily price movement of an index consisting of upstream exploration and production companies along with refiners. However, there are a few exchange-traded products that aim to deliver double the daily price movements of a broader energy equity index:

Exchange-Traded Product

Underlying Index

Assets Under Management

Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF

S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index

$236.29 million

Direxion Daily Energy Bull 2X Shares ETF (ERX)

Energy Select Sector Index

$251.62 million

Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bear 2X Shares ETF

S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index

$37.51 million

Direxion Daily Energy Bear 2X Shares (ERY)

Energy Select Sector Index

$17.70 million

MicroSectors U.S. Big Oil 3x Leveraged ETNs (NRGU)

Solactive MicroSectors U.S. Big Oil Index

$29.69 million

MicroSectors U.S. Big Oil -3X Inverse Leveraged ETNs (NRGD)

Solactive MicroSectors U.S. Big Oil Index

$2.18 million

MicroSectors Oil & Gas Exploration & Production 3X Leveraged ETNs (OILU)

MicroSectors Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Index

$40.09 million

MicroSectors Oil & Gas Exploration & Production -3X Inverse Leveraged ETN (OILD)

MicroSectors Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Index

$8.50 million

MicroSectors Energy 3X Leveraged ETNs (WTIU)

Solactive MicroSectors Energy Index

$15.84 million

MicroSectors Energy -3X Inverse Leveraged ETNs (WTID)

Solactive MicroSectors Energy Index

$1.61 million

One thing that we should note here is that all the MicroSectors products are exchange-traded notes, which are different from exchange-traded funds. An exchange-traded note is a bond under which the bond issuer has agreed to pay the bondholders an amount corresponding to the return of a specified asset (in this case, the specified return of an index, compounded daily). An exchange-traded note does not actually hold any underlying assets, and the taxation rules can be different from those of an exchange-traded fund. Furthermore, there is the possibility that the issuer of the exchange-traded note will default. As such, they are not perfectly comparable to exchange-traded funds such as GUSH.

We also see that all of the above securities use different indices as their benchmark. As such, the equities that comprise each index are not necessarily the same, so their performances will not necessarily be identical. Finally, the MicroSectors products aim to deliver 300% of the daily price movements of their respective indices rather than 200% of the daily price movements, as is the case with the Direxion products. As such, the MicroSectors products will be more volatile than the Direxion products. All of these products are designed to be used by traders looking to capitalize on intraday price movements, and not long-term investors looking to grow their wealth. As such, volatility might not be a problem for anyone who is considering using these products as they are intended, but it is still an important thing to be aware of.

The State Street SPDR S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production ETF uses the same benchmark index as the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF, but it does not include the leverage. As such, investors who want to make a profit from a thesis that could take multiple days to play out may be better off using this ETF instead of GUSH. Its movements over a multi-day period will more closely correlate with the benchmark index than will GUSH’s movements.

The Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF has a 2.30% yield at the January 21, 2026, closing price. This is actually lower than the 2.45% yield of the XOP ETF. GUSH is not designed for income investors, as it suffers from net asset value decay over time, and the distribution will not offset the falling net asset value over time.

About the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index

As has been stated a few times throughout this article, the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF aims to deliver 200% of the price movement of the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index on a daily basis. As such, we should take a look at how the index is designed and structured to further our understanding of how this fund may perform in real-world situations.

The website for the index states:

S&P Select Industry Indices are designed to measure the performance of narrow GICS sub-industries. The S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index comprises stocks in the S&P Total Market Index that are classified in the GICS Integrated Oil & Gas, Oil & Gas Exploration & Production and Oil & Gas Refining & Marketing sub-industries.

This summary of the index also provides some information about how it is constructed. First, every company that is included in the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index must also be included in the S&P Total Market Index. The website for the S&P Total Market Index provides a brief description of this index:

The S&P Total Market Index is designed to track the broad equity market, including large-, mid-, small-, and micro-cap stocks. The S&P 500 and the S&P Completion Index are subsets of the S&P TMI.

The S&P Total Market Index is one of a few indices that are designed to track the performance of every publicly traded stock in the United States. A few other indices that claim to have the same goal are the Wilshire 5000 and the MSCI Investable Market Index. Naturally, they all use a slightly different methodology with respect to their construction, and when we consider that all of these indices have a different number of constituent companies, it is questionable whether or not any of them actually include every publicly traded company in the United States. For its part, the S&P Total Market Index includes 3,879 constituents with market capitalizations ranging from $120,000 to $4.531950 trillion (market capitalization numbers as of December 31, 2025):

The S&P Total Market Index has the same reconstitution and rebalancing schedule as the S&P 500 Index (SP500). The index is a float-adjusted market-capitalization-weighted index (so the larger the company’s market capitalization, the larger its weighting in the index) that is rebalanced on the third Friday of March, June, September, and December.

The S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index follows the same rebalancing schedule as the S&P Total Market Index and the S&P 500 Index. However, it is an equal-weighted index in which all companies are set to have the same weighting on each rebalancing date. This has the effect of preventing situations like the one that regularly occurs with the S&P 500 whenever a handful of large companies basically determine the performance of the entire index. If this index were market capitalization-weighted, then the performance of ExxonMobil and Chevron would basically determine the performance of the entire index. As an equal-weighted index, this is not the case.

The Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF does not simply purchase the common equities that comprise the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index because of the need for the fund to deliver 200% of the index’s daily movements. We will discuss the exact holdings of this ETF in a few moments.

The S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index consists of the common equity of any company that is in the S&P 500 Total Market Index and is included in one of the following three industries:

  1. Integrated Oil & Gas—This is a company that operates in all the different subsectors of the oil and gas industry. An integrated company will usually have upstream operations that extract the hydrocarbons from the ground as well as refining operations. Companies such as Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM) or Chevron Corporation (CVX) are integrated oil & gas companies.
  2. Oil & Gas Exploration & Production—This is a company that finds new sources of oil and gas and then drills the wells that extract the resources from the ground. They do not own refineries or processing plants, and they do not sell directly to end users of the products. Companies such as Diamondback Energy, Inc. (FANG) or EQT Corporation (EQT) are exploration and production companies.
  3. Oil & Gas Refining & Marketing – This is a company that purchases crude oil and converts it into gasoline, kerosene, jet fuel, diesel fuel, or similar products that are consumed by energy users. These companies may also sell directly to end users of energy products. Companies such as Valero Energy Corporation (VLO) or Phillips 66 (PSX) are refining and marketing companies.

The inclusion of refining and marketing companies is perhaps a curious choice, as these companies have a different business model than exploration & production companies. An exploration & production company pulls hydrocarbon resources directly out of the ground and sells them, so its revenues are directly correlated with crude oil prices, natural gas prices, or both. A refinery, however, purchases crude oil, refines it, and sells the refined products. Thus, its cash flows depend on the difference between the price of crude oil and the price of refined products. As such, refineries are not necessarily hurt when oil prices fall as exploration and production companies are. In fact, refineries can benefit from falling oil prices because their margins increase, which strengthens their profitability.

The largest companies in the index consist of a mixture of crude oil and natural gas producers, as well as refiners:

The index does include ExxonMobil and Chevron, but both companies have lower weightings than the largest companies in the index. This is possible despite the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index being an equal-weight index. The reason for this is that the index is only rebalanced quarterly, so the weightings will naturally fluctuate between the rebalancing dates as their individual stocks deliver different performances.

Due to this, predicting the movements of the index is not as simple as simply assuming a positive correlation to crude oil prices. This chart shows the spot price performance of West Texas Intermediate crude oil alongside the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index over the five-year period that ended on January 22, 2026:

In this chart, the black line is the spot price of West Texas Intermediate crude oil. The blue line is the XOP ETF that tracks the index. As we can see, the index usually rises whenever the price of crude oil rises and declines whenever the price of crude oil declines. It is not a perfect correlation, however, as the price of oil ended the period flat (albeit with a lot of volatility), whereas the index was up 72.10%. This could suggest that GUSH will probably rise on a day that crude oil prices are rising, and vice versa, but it is not guaranteed to be the case.

Traders who are considering using GUSH as a way to profit from oil price movements are advised to keep in mind that this ETF is aiming to deliver double the daily price movement of an index of the common equities of companies that operate in the oil and gas sector. It does not attempt to deliver a performance that correlates with crude oil itself, and its performance might not necessarily be exactly as expected based on oil price movements.

Composition of the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2x Shares ETF

The standard strategy that many ETFs use if they are attempting to match the performance of an index is to simply purchase all the securities in the index with the same weightings as the index itself possesses. However, GUSH is aiming to deliver double the daily price movement of its benchmark index, so that strategy will not work. Rather, the fund will need to use swaps, futures, or other derivative products that have built-in leverage to achieve its goal. The prospectus for GUSH states:

The Fund, under normal circumstances, invests at least 80% of its net assets in financial instruments, such as swap agreements, securities of the Index, and exchange-traded funds that track the Index, that, in combination, provide 2X daily leveraged exposure to the Index, consistent with the Fund’s investment objective. The financial instruments in which the Fund most commonly invests are swap agreements and futures agreements which are intended to produce economically leveraged investment results.

The final sentence of this quote explicitly states that the fund will usually be invested in swap agreements or futures, which allow it to deliver double the performance of the index on any given day. Futures contracts can be quite useful for this because they allow the fund to essentially control more assets than it could if it purchased the equities in the index. This is necessary given that the goal is to deliver double the performance of the index.

A full list of the fund’s holdings can be found here. As of January 22, 2026, the fund’s holdings consist of several of the common equities that comprise the index, along with a number of swap contracts:

A swap contract is an agreement with a counterparty to exchange the cash flows provided by two different financial instruments. These all appear to be index swaps, which usually are used for one party to obtain exposure to the performance of a given index without owning the securities in the index. Normally, one party will pay a fixed amount while the counterparty pays a variable rate that is dependent on the return of an index. These swaps can be highly customized, but it seems that the fund is using them to get an amount of money that depends on the performance of the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Index. When combined with the securities in the index that the fund also owns, the payments that it receives from these swaps allow it to deliver greater than the performance of the index. It is unclear what the fund is paying to the counterparty to these swaps, but it is probably a specified amount of money that is based on some benchmark interest rate. The fund had 5.45% of its net assets invested in an institutional U.S. Treasury money market fund on January 22, 2026, so it is possible that it is using the cash generated by this to cover its obligations under the swaps.

The fund resets its portfolio daily, which results in it realizing both gains and losses. This is the reason why it does not accomplish its objective if held overnight. The fund needs to do this so that its daily movements correspond to double that of the index. The compounding effects of the daily realization of gains and losses are also the cause of the fund’s net asset value decay over time.

We can see how the daily realization of gains and losses works with a simple example. Let us assume that on Day 1, the index and the fund both start at $100. The index goes up 10% to $110. We would assume that the fund would go up 20%, which puts it at $120. On Day 2, the index goes down 9.09%, which puts it back at $100. The index is basically flat over the two days. However, the fund would go down 18.18%, which is a $21.82 decline from its Day 2 starting point of $120. That puts the fund at $98.18. Over the two-day period, the index is flat, but the fund is down. As this goes on day after day, the fund will lose money even if the index ends up flat overall. This is one big reason why this fund should not be held overnight, as there is a very high probability of losing money over time.

Expenses

The website for the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF states that the fund has an expense ratio of 0.93%.

Conclusion

In conclusion, the Direxion Daily S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Bull 2X Shares ETF is a leveraged exchange-traded fund that aims to deliver double the daily performance of the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index. The fund uses a combination of equities and derivatives to achieve its goal, but due to the daily reset, it does not deliver double the performance of the index over a multi-day period. The fund is not intended to be held for an extended period, and it is most suited for a short-term trader who plans to buy and sell the ETF within the same day. An investor looking to profit off a thesis that will take several days to play out could be better off with an unleveraged ETF that is designed for long-term investment.

This article answers three main questions about GUSH:

  1. What can be found in GUSH's portfolio?
  2. How does GUSH relate to the S&P Oil & Gas Exploration & Production Select Industry Index?
  3. What risks apply to using GUSH in a portfolio?

Editor's note: This article is intended to provide a general overview of the ETF for educational purposes only and, unlike other articles on Seeking Alpha, does not offer an investment opinion about the ETF.

data.title: Las Vegas Sands: 3 Things To Look For In Next Week's Earnings Report
data.description:
Introduction and Investor Takeaway

Las Vegas Sands Corp. (LVS) business has been showing strength in Singapore and weakness in Macao as of late. Notwithstanding the mixed performance, the shares are relatively undervalued and, in this article, we identify important drivers that we will be paying attention to in next week’s earnings report to form our thesis. For their business in Singapore, we will be looking for continued momentum in the hold percentage, which is due to the uptake of proposition bets and the continued rollout of smart table technology. For their business in Macao, we will be paying attention to visitation levels from the lower GDP-per-capita provinces in China, and evidence that the current management strategy for Macao is working. Evidence of the strategy working might come in the form of stable or minimal EBITDA margin deterioration for Macao. Trends in the hold percentage, visitation among lower GDP-per-capita provinces in China, and the EBITDA margins for their Macao business are the three items we believe underpin their current operating performance.

In my previous article, we issued a buy rating on LVS with a price target of $54 based on a price-to-sales (P/S) ratio of 3.5x. While the price has surpassed the price target of $54, it is below the 3.5x price-to-sales ratio, given the upward revisions in earnings and revenue estimates since my last article. We see no reason to make haste in changing my recommendation, given where the stock price is and the time period to the upcoming fiscal year 2025 report.

In this paper, we review the recent operating performance of LVS and connect the dots between the operating performance and the items that we will be paying close attention to.

Asset Turnover

The asset turnover ratio measures sales per dollar of assets. The stronger the asset, the more revenue it will generate, and the weaker the asset, the less revenue it will bring in.

Here is the next CEO of LVS describing their business model:

“…we start by building a great customer experience by focusing on the physical asset, which took time to both design and ultimately implement…we focused on a lot of different amenities, how we enhanced our entertainment, how we enhance our retail mall, how we enhance our food and beverage and how we bring it all together so that gaming customers can come in and get a lifestyle experience that can’t be replicated any place else”.

Source: Current COO and Director Patrick Dumont, LVS Earnings Call Q3, 2025.

A starting point to gauge the health of LVS’s business is to review the total asset turnover ratio. Please see Exhibit 1.

The bar for 2025 is colored differently because it is an estimate, as fiscal 2025 figures are not yet published. We used the trailing twelve months for revenue and the last reported total asset figure in arriving at our 2025 estimate.

After Covid-related underperformance, the company seems to be getting its groove back. Let us narrow our focus and look at net property, plant & equipment as the denominator. Please see Exhibit 2.

Unsurprisingly, the exhibit looks similar to the preceding one. LVS’s competitive edge is its physical assets.

LVS conducts business in two geographic areas. In Singapore, they have one integrated resort, the Marina Bay Sands (MBS), and in Macao, they have five integrated resorts. Let us narrow our focus and look at the smaller of the two geographies. Please see Exhibit 3.

The Marina Bay Sands in Singapore seems to be doing well. Mass gaming revenues show a clear uptrend, comparing the recent quarter to Q3 2024 and Q3 2023. Let us dig a little deeper to identify the potential source of the outperformance.

Baccarat is the dominant form of table game in Asia and is the game from which LVS derives most of its revenue. The prevalence of baccarat as the game of choice for patrons is not accidental and has to do with the relative simplicity of the game, the social aspect (players bet on either of the two hands on the table), and the low house edge. For baccarat, the hold, which is the percentage of the amount wagered that LVS “holds” and roughly corresponds to the revenues that they book, has historically been around 2.85%. For Q3 2025, LVS's actual hold percentage for MBS was 4.84%. The increase in hold does not have any corresponding increase in capex or expense; most of it drops to the bottom line. Please see Exhibit 4 for the expected hold rates for recent quarters. The expected hold differs from the actual hold because you cannot predict precisely the outcome of the game and amounts won or lost beforehand.

The increase in hold has to do with the introduction of side bets or proposition bets. Just as in football or in sports betting, bettors can wager on an event within the game that is not necessarily tied to the outcome of the game; in MBS, a bet can be placed on an event within the game of baccarat. The house edge on the side bets is much higher than 2.85%.

LVS has also introduced “smart tables” in MBS. The smart table doesn’t influence gaming decisions, but does give LVS a better ability to measure the number of bets and the odds of the proposition bets offered or being considered.

Continued momentum in the hold percentage for MBS and the extent that they are rolling out side bets in Macao will be something we will be listening to in next week’s earnings call. It could be an important tailwind for the business.

Macao

Please see Exhibit 5.

For Macao, the picture is not very encouraging. Even the performance of the Londoner has to be put in context. In November 2023, LVS began the Londoner phase 2 renovation, which took suites and floor space out of service until Q2 2025, and therefore, the apparent outperformance of Q3 2025 versus Q3 2024 is impacted by having more room inventory available.

The weakness in Macao is related to the visitation figures from lower GDP-per-capita provinces. Please see Exhibit 6.

The provinces of Beijing, Shanghai, and Jiangsu are among the highest GDP-per-capita provinces in China, and the provinces of Jilin, Guangxi, and Heilongjiang have some of the lowest GDP-per-capita provinces.

Higher GDP-per-capita provinces have visitation numbers that exceed their prepandemic levels, but the visitation among lower GDP-per-capita provinces is still below their 2019 levels. Until visitation from lower GDP-per-capita provinces meets or exceeds their pre-COVID figures, it is difficult to make the case that LVS is running on all cylinders.

EBITDA margin

The weakness in Macao has prompted the management team at LVS to pursue a more promotional strategy in recent months. In essence, the management team is sacrificing margin with the hope that it can induce more traffic from lower GDP-per-capita provinces. For Q3 2025, the adjusted property EBITDA margin for Macao was 150 basis points lower than Q3 2024. Please look at Exhibits 7 and 8.

The EBITDA margin for MBS is stable and hovers around a very healthy 50%. For Macao, the EBITDA margin is in the low 30% range. Any color that the management team may provide in next week’s call on EBITDA margins and foot traffic in Macao could be significant.

Valuation

In our previous article, we issued a buy recommendation on the stock with a price target of $54 based on a P/S multiple of 3.5x. The P/S ratio is our preferred metric in evaluating LVS’s stock. James O’Shaughnessy, in his book "What Works on Wall Street," provided evidence that a low P/S multiple is the most useful metric for predicting future returns. We believe that the ratio is appropriate because it is the quality of the assets that creates memorable guest experiences, which will ultimately drive LVS's revenues. As long as there is no fundamental departure in the asset quality or the historical capital structure, we believe the P/S ratio can be relied on.

Source: O’Shaughnessy, James P. What Works on Wall Street (1998), McGraw-Hill

Please see Exhibit 9.

The exhibit depicts a rising asset turnover ratio and a muted price-to-sales ratio.

Just to put a finer point on this, since our previous article, LVS beat on the top and bottom line in Q2 and Q3. Consensus revenue is now expected to come in at $12.68 billion and $13.39 billion for fiscal 2025 and 2026, respectively. Please see Table 1, where we use these figures as our starting point.

Table 1: Price-to-Sales Ratio for LVS
2025 2026
Consensus revenue($ in billions) 12.68 13.39
Shares outstanding (billions) 0.704 0.704
Revenue per share $ 18.01 $ 19.02
Price-to-sales ratio 3.5 3.5
Implied valuation $ 63.04 $ 66.57

Source: Seeking Alpha, LVS Q3 2025 Earnings Presentation and Author's calculation

Given the revenue figures and holding the current shares outstanding constant, we arrive at a revenue per share of $18.01 and $19.02 for 2025 and 2026. Using a 3.5x price-to-sales multiple, we arrive at a share price that is above the current quotation, suggesting a slight margin of safety is present. Moreover, the implied valuation for 2026 is likely understated because we are assuming the current share count as an appropriate input for the implied value for 2026. LVS has been on a share buyback spree and has repurchased 11.5% of outstanding shares over the last eight quarters.

Risks

We believe that there are at least two important risks that readers should consider. The first is that the expansion of the hold percentage in MBS could mean revert. We have inferred that the hold percentage would be “higher for longer” because of the recent introduction of side bets. However, it is possible that the popularity of side bets wanes and that the hold percentage returns to the historical average, which would curtail revenues and profitability. Secondly, there is a presumption that visitation from lower GDP-per-capita provinces in China will return to their pre-COVID levels at some point. This presumption may be flawed – things could get worse. Visitation could drop. Or it could take a lot longer than we expect for visitation to recover. Any of these items would pose a material headwind for LVS and could alter our view on the stock.

Conclusion

In this article, we highlighted a strength and a weakness of LVS’s business. The strength is from the performance of their integrated resort in Singapore, and the weakness has to do with a segment of the visitor population of their Macao resorts not yet displaying similar habits to the pre-COVID-19 time period. The management team is attempting to shore up business in Macao with promotional activities that will probably contract EBITDA margins. Having highlighted the recent operating performance of LVS, we do believe that, on balance, there exists an asymmetry of risk and return at the current quotation and that there is room for the valuation to expand further, even with the headwinds in Macao. LVS doesn’t have to be operating perfectly for its shares to be a buy. It just has to be operating better than the market gives it credit for.

data.title: Celcuity Inc.: A Die To Be Cast Before July 2026
data.description:
Topline Summary and Update

Celcuity Inc.'s (CELC) rapid ascension to a mature valuation (just over $5 billion in market cap as I write this) comes only a few months after my last coverage of the company back in October, where I elaborated on what I saw as optimistic signals for their breast cancer program. Since then, Seeking Alpha analysts have been unanimous in their favorable outlooks heading into 2026. Now, we begin 2026 with yet another surge in material events, and I want to take another look at CELC to give a sense of why I think they're still worthy of a tentative "Buy" sentiment even as they've continued to climb ever higher.

Pipeline Updates

Gedatolisib

The lone pipeline candidate being developed by CELC is the gedatolisib, an inhibitor of PI3K and MTORC1/2 that blocks activity of the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway (abbreviated PAM). This is an important pathway target for exploitation in the setting of breast cancer, where agents like capivasertib have been an established treatment option for years. In fact, PI3K blockade has been a hotly explored target for a very long time, but tolerability issues have made the road very bumpy for pioneers like idelalisib and duvelisib.

Gedatolisib is thought to be different from approved options like capivasertib because of its wide range of activity against players in this PAM pathway, which appears to be associated with improved activity against hormone receptor HR-positive breast cancer without mutations in PIK3CA, the gene that encodes for a key component of PI3K. Capivasertib, for reference, is only active (and approved) in the setting of PIK3CA-mutated disease (or a similar alteration).

On the science front, the main update since my last coverage of the company was the presentation of updated findings from the phase 3 VIKTORIA-1 trial at SABCS 2025. We knew that gedatolisib, when added to fulvestrant, was better than fulvestrant alone in the setting of previously treated HR-positive breast cancer, but this was a deeper dive into the magnitude of benefit.

Specifically, patients with a long time to progression on prior therapy (no progression for longer than 18 months), gedatolisib plus fulvestrant yielded a median progression-free survival of 10 months compared with 1.9 months for fulvestrant alone. Adding the CDK4/6 inhibitor to this doublet yielded a median progression-free survival of 12.4 months. The analysis also showcased success with mitigating grade 2 or 3 stomatitis, as well as improved time to deterioration of quality of life with the use of gedatolisib. Unlike with capivasertib, gedatolisib was not associated with hyperglycemia.

Around the same time, CELC announced that they had a new drug application with the FDA submitted, and the most recent news for the company (at the time of writing) was the announced acceptance of this application by the FDA, with a PDUFA action date set for July 17, 2026. This means that the FDA will be rendering its decision on or before that date.

Financial Overview

At the end of the last quarter, CELC held $74.2 million in cash and equivalents, as well as $380.7 million in investments. They also held $125.2 million and $195.1 million in a non-current note payable and convertible debt, respectively.

Operating expenses for the quarter were $42.8 million, and after interest, the net loss for the quarter was $43.8 million. For the first 9 months of 2025, the actual cash burn was $116.9 million (around $39 million per quarter). At this burn rate, the company has between 11 and 12 quarters of liquidity to fund operations while keeping in mind the unpredictable costs associated with launching gedatolisib, assuming approval.

Strengths and Risks

Strength - Strong likelihood of a drug launch in 2026

With the results of VIKTORIA-1 in hand, it looks about as likely as anything to me that gedatolisib is going to be approved by the midpoint of the year or so. It would not surprise me to see a decision rendered a bit early, in fact.

And the market's enthusiasm is quite justified, in my opinion. Capivasertib is approved in a somewhat similar indication, but with the limitation of being gated by a biomarker (PIK3CA or similar alteration must be present), and even with that, AstraZeneca believes capivasertib could have peak sales topping $1.2 billion annually. If they're given no restriction, then the original ~$2.4 billion peak sales estimate could well be on the table for gedatolisib, and those are numbers worth getting excited about.

Risk - High expectations and reality often don't mix

Unfortunately, we also need to contend with the fact that the market is pricing in a lot of success for CELC right now as it stands. I like to take a company like Exelixis as a comparator, given that it has a successful launch of a single drug (although across multiple indications), and use its price-to-sales ratio, which stands around 5.3 (based on the latest quarterly filing). Disregarding current liquidity, other assets, and liabilities, with a share price of approximately $110 and a similar price-to-sales ratio, the market is essentially pricing in just about $900-$950 million in revenue.

This, to me, is a signal that the market is pricing in approval, a successful launch, and meeting pretty aggressive sales growth metrics. Of course, CELC does have liquidity to take into account, and they have a long list of other indications being explored for gedatolisib, including (but not limited to):

  • Frontline management of HR-positive breast cancer (VIKTORIA-2 trial)
  • Castration-resistant prostate cancer (CELC-G-201 trial)
  • Endometrial cancer

Each of these could add substantial value and may justify the premium being built into the CELC stock price at this time.

Bottom-Line Summary

Unfortunately, even though the news has been quite good for CELC as they move toward becoming a commercial company for the first time, the market is also running hot on the stock. Having more than doubled since my last article, CELC is a bit harder to hold a "Buy" sentiment on, considering the level of success that you could argue is being priced into the equation right now. If they are able to reach peak sales above $2 billion, then there's obvious upside here, but that assumes they're able to successfully navigate the execution risks, as well as growing competition from other agents being developed in the setting of HR-positive breast cancer.

However, there is still too much reason for optimism for me to feel a downgrade of sentiment is warranted. I will be reaffirming a "Buy" here, in the end, with the caveat that in the near term you're likely to find a price point that serves as a better entry. But there's a lot of reason to be excited, and I look forward to seeing what else CELC can bring to the table in 2026.

data.title: Oil May Be Heading For A Breakout
data.description:
Oil has been the one commodity these days that can't seem to catch a bid, but that may be about to change.

Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and Silver (XAGUSD:CUR) have been among the hottest commodities globally, along with plenty of other metals. Of course, oil faces supply and demand issues more than some metals, and that could be one reason crude oil prices (CO1:COM, CL1:COM) have lagged.

Technicals Are Improving

From a technical point of view, oil is showing significant improvement after finding long-term support around $55 and, more recently, making a solid move higher. Oil has cleared a downtrend that started back in July, and is now testing a resistance level at $61. A breakout and push beyond $61 could help propel oil back to around $65, at least in the near term, and, more importantly, be the start of something more.

Additionally, momentum has improved as measured by the relative strength index. The recent "breakout" in the momentum indicator may signal that, after a long struggle, oil is finally building the momentum to push higher.

Crude oil is also very close to breaking above its 200-day moving average, which, for the time being, has been a region the commodity has struggled to move beyond on several occasions since 2025.

Positioning Has Turned Bearish

Over the last year, non-commercial traders have seen their long positions fall to their lowest levels in some time, while short positions have been gradually building. Non-commercial traders betting on the price declining may be right, but it would also mean oil would probably need to fall below $55. Again, the technical chart shows that the support level has been tested several times over the past year, with no break yet. If Oil does start to build some momentum, the shorts could find themselves needing to cover, and with long positions so low, it is possible that oil could eventually squeeze higher.

Trend-following systematic funds, such as commodity trading advisors, are likely short oil at this point, given that the 20-day moving average is below the 120-day moving average. Typically, these funds use moving averages, such as the 20-day and 120-day, to determine the trend. However, more recently, the 20-day moving average has started to turn higher. If it crosses above the 120-day moving average, it could trigger these CTA funds to start unwinding their short positions and eventually go long.

It isn't a hard-and-fast rule, because every fund can differ, but it can serve as a rule of thumb. Since the beginning of 2025, when the 20-day moving average fell below the 120 moving average, non-commercial short positions have been trending higher, while long positions have been declining.

Risks To The Bullish Thesis

One issue for oil is the dollar (DXY). Typically, a stronger dollar pushes oil prices lower, and a weaker dollar makes them higher, but this is not a certainty.

On a 1-year rolling correlation, the relationship between them has been inverse; however, more recently, it has turned positive, with oil and the dollar showing a positive correlation, meaning they have at times risen together, such as in 2022.

So it remains possible that oil and the dollar can rise together, though it is not common.

Other risks for oil at this point are obviously supply. With leadership changes in Venezuela and prospects for more supply coming online, it could help drive oil prices lower, especially if it happens sooner than expected. Risk of a global slowdown or recession is always present, even though, currently, a recession, at least in the U.S., does not appear likely, given the recent strong third-quarter GDP report.

The technicals do appear to be improving for oil, and that has put it on the cusp of breaking out and moving higher. It isn't there yet, and still has work to do, but with gold and plenty of other commodities running, it may be oil's turn soon enough.

data.title: CSX Corporation Q4: Lackluster End To The Year, But 2026 Looks To Be Better
data.description:
Introduction

CSX Corporation (CSX) reported what I would consider a lackluster end to the year, with a bit more optimistic outlook for 2026 on the back of promising cost-cutting initiatives rather than a better, broader outlook, which helped its shares pass the subpar 2025 performance in hopes of better profitability is yet to come.

I am not too keen on adding CXS to my portfolio just yet, as I still find it a bit expensive, but the cash flow generation looks to be very promising, and will keep an eye out going forward.

By The Numbers

Starting from the top, revenue came in at around $3.51B, essentially flat y/y (-0.8%) and $30m below estimates. Such weak performance came from “lower merchandise volume and reduced export coal revenue.” Some of the negatives were offset by an increase in intermodal revenue growth and higher fuel pricing/fuel surcharges.

Intermodal volumes rose 5%, while revenue was up 7% on new business. Coal volumes were up slightly by 1%. So, overall, a lackluster top-line performance.

On to the company’s profitability and efficiency, operating income came in at $1.11B, down from $1.21B a year ago. And EPS came in at $0.39 vs $0.42 a year ago, affected by $50m in severance and other tech costs. Operating margins overall were up around 30bps from last year.

Let’s take a look at the company’s financial position.
